name: V8 Orinoco — Hybrid Generational Concurrent
summary: V8's Orinoco combines parallel, concurrent, and incremental techniques across a generational heap (Scavenger young-gen + Mark-Compact major) to push GC work off the main JS thread.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: V8's **Orinoco** combines parallel, concurrent, and incremental techniques across a generational heap (Scavenger young-gen + Mark-Compact major) to push GC work off the main JS thread. Default in Chrome / Node.js; stable production since ~2017.

sections.title: Concurrent marking and parallel scavenging
sections.content:
**Concurrent marking** with helper threads + write-barrier-tracked refs; main thread does only a brief marking-finalization pause. **Parallel Scavenger** and **parallel compaction** reduced compaction time from ~7 ms to under 2 ms; concurrent sweeping runs alongside JS execution.

Black allocation, parallel remembered-set processing, and **Oilpan integration** for Blink/C++ tracing across the JS heap boundary. Oilpan is what makes V8's GC reach into Chrome's DOM/C++ world without a separate collector for each side.

sections.title: Deployment and significance
sections.content:
Default in Chrome / Node.js; stable production since ~2017.

Orinoco is the largest-deployment example of "hybrid" GC design: rather than picking parallel vs. concurrent vs. incremental, V8 uses each technique where it pays off best — Scavenger is parallel STW (young heap, short pauses), major marking is concurrent (background threads), compaction is parallel STW (with concurrent sweeping). The combination keeps the JS main thread responsive even under heavy allocation.
